# Long Task Summary

## Original Goal

Upgrade toktra according to the five-layer architecture recommendation, then review the result layer by layer.

## Final Artifact

An upgraded Chrome MV3 translation extension with explicit Segmenter, Scheduler, Provider, Render, and Rule/AI Strategy layers.

## Executive Summary

toktra now treats page translation as structured work rather than a direct text-batch loop. The content script collects segment metadata, plans cache-first and viewport-aware translation work, sends ID-keyed segment requests to the background provider layer, renders translations plus progress state safely, and supplements the default walker with user site rules and AI strategies.

The latest iteration adds PDF translation through a toktra-owned PDF viewer and changes page/PDF scheduling to translate only the current screen plus the next two screens until the user scrolls.

## Timeline

| Phase | What happened | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Bootstrap | Initialized longtask docs and state. | `.longtask/run.json` |
| Requirements | Defined P0/P1 acceptance criteria for five layers. | `docs/AcceptanceCriteria.md` |
| Benchmark | Mapped open-source references to toktra decisions. | `docs/BenchmarkReport.md` |
| Architecture | Documented contracts and data models. | `docs/Architecture.md` |
| Implementation | Added tests, watched them fail, implemented the layers, and revalidated. | `tests/*.test.js`, `extension/*.js` |
| Validation | Ran automated tests, lint, and TDWH HTML sampling. | `docs/EvalReport.md` |

## Major Decisions

| Decision | Reason | Alternatives considered | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| Keep bilingual insertion | Avoid HTML alignment and app state breakage. | In-place replacement | Safer rendering and easier restore. |
| Add segment IDs | Needed stable mapping for parallel requests. | Rely on array order only | Less fragile provider responses. |
| Namespace cache | Different providers/models/prompts can return different output. | Text-only cache key | Prevents stale translation reuse. |
| Add site rules as additive strategy | Rules should fix misses without disabling default walker. | Rule-only page selection | Better coverage resilience. |

## Problems Encountered

| Problem | Root cause | Fix | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Missing five-layer primitives | Existing code was a compact direct pipeline. | Added explicit core/background contracts. | `npm test` |
| Scheduler could prioritize headings over top DOM when layout is absent | Priority tie-breaker was too strong. | Visual/DOM order now wins before priority. | TDWH sampling first ten texts follow top-page order. |
| Progress removal count included injected style | `renderProgress()` injects toktra style. | Corrected test expectation while still asserting progress is removed. | `npm test` |

## Highlights

### Product Highlights

- Users can see translation progress instead of guessing whether work is still running.
- Site rules can target recurring misses without waiting for AI strategy.

### Engineering Highlights

- Segment targets now carry `id`, `kind`, `priority`, and `position`.
- Provider layer supports ID-keyed segment results while preserving old text-array API.
- Cache keys now include provider/model/language/prompt namespace.

### UI / Aesthetic Highlights

- Progress UI is a small fixed pill, not a modal or heavy toolbar.

### Automation / Workflow Highlights

- Added regression tests before implementation and validated with `npm test` and `npm run lint`.
